q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: etree.fromstring called with parser configured with resolve_entities=True, load_dtd=True, no_network=False, enabling external entity resolution (network I/O, file access)

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: not evaluated, decision stopped at Q1

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: not evaluated, decision stopped at Q1

mock_guidance.target: Mock etree.XMLParser on line 192 to override unsafe defaults, or mock etree.fromstring on line 193 to prevent actual parsing
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the request.body argument passed to etree.fromstring to verify it reaches the sink
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a safe dummy lxml Element (e.g., etree.Element('root')) so downstream access to doc.tag in the JsonResponse succeeds without triggering entity resolution
notes: Mocking XMLParser with safe defaults (resolve_entities=False, load_dtd=False) is an alternative that still exercises real parsing logic
If mocking fromstring directly, ensure the fake element has a .tag attribute since the function returns JsonResponse({'root': doc.tag})
